general_farm_questions.type_of_farming_line_1: Mixed fruit and vegetable operation with a focus on berries (strawberries, blueberries, and blackberries) and seasonal produce such as sweet corn, tomatoes, squash, cucumbers, and pumpkins. About 20 acres are devoted to rotating vegetable crops using raised beds, drip irrigation, and cover cropping to maintain soil health.
general_farm_questions.type_of_farming_line_2: Small-scale livestock and poultry, including a flock of ~45 laying hens for farm-fresh eggs and a few pygmy goats used primarily for agritourism (petting zoo and pasture management) rather than meat production. We also keep two beehives for pollination and small-batch honey sold directly to visitors.
general_farm_questions.type_of_farming_line_3: Agritourism-focused activities such as U-pick berries and pumpkins, guided educational tours for school groups on sustainable farming and composting, a small farmstand with value-added products (jams, pickles, and baked goods made off-site in a certified kitchen), and seasonal events like fall festivals and farm-to-table dinners.

agritourism_profile.open_for_business_line_1: Regular season hours (April 1–August 31): Wednesday–Friday 10:00–18:00, Saturday 09:00–18:00, Sunday 12:00–17:00; closed Monday–Tuesday except for pre-booked group tours.
agritourism_profile.open_for_business_line_2: Fall season (September 15–October 31): Tuesday–Friday 10:00–18:00, Saturday 09:00–19:00, Sunday 12:00–18:00; evening campfire and hayride packages by reservation on Fridays and Saturdays 18:30–21:00.
agritourism_profile.open_for_business_line_3: Winter season (November 15–December 20): Farmstand open Saturdays only 10:00–15:00 for holiday gift baskets and storage crops; additional weekday hours available for school field trips and private group tours by advance reservation.
agritourism_profile.special_events_line_1: Annual Spring Berry Festival – held the first full weekend in May each year (for example, 2025-05-03 and 2025-05-04), 10:00–17:00 both days, featuring live local music, food trucks, children’s activities, U-pick strawberries, and guided farm walks focusing on pollinators and early-season planting.
agritourism_profile.special_events_line_2: Harvest & Pumpkin Patch Weekend Series – every Saturday and Sunday from 2025-10-04 through 2025-10-26, 10:00–18:00, with corn maze, hayrides, U-pick pumpkins, goat petting area, farm education stations, and hourly mini-workshops on composting, backyard gardening, and cooking with seasonal produce.
agritourism_profile.special_events_line_3: Farm-to-Table Dinner Nights – three Saturdays each year (for example, 2025-06-14, 2025-09-13, and 2025-10-18), 18:00–21:00, featuring a multi-course meal served outdoors using primarily farm-grown produce, live acoustic music, a guided sunset farm tour, and a short talk by the farmer about sustainable practices and the history of the farm.
